briefdescription.para: Represents all possible states.
No state (0)
View has keyboard focus.
View is being pressed (touch or key)
View is disabled.
View appears disabled but still interactive.
View is selected.
SELECTED + PRESSED.
DISABLED + SELECTED.
SELECTED + FOCUSED.
Creates a Normal state (no bits set).
Checks if this state fully contains the specified state.

simplesect.para: True if all bits of other are set in this state

briefdescription.para: Checks if there is any overlap between this and the other state.

simplesect.para: True if any common bit is set

briefdescription.para: Checks if this is the Normal state (no bits set).

simplesect.para: True if no state bits are set

briefdescription.para: Checks if this state contains DISABLED or PSEUDO_DISABLED.

simplesect.para: True if either DISABLED or PSEUDO_DISABLED is set

briefdescription.para: Returns a human-readable string representation for debugging.
Checks if this state was newly added (not in prev, present in cur).
Checks if this state was removed (present in prev, not in cur).
Checks if this state was either added or removed.
Checks if any bit in this state changed between prev and cur.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike WasChanged(), this works with composite states — it returns true if any constituent bit was toggled.

briefdescription.para: Registers and creates a custom state with the given name.

detaileddescription.para: If the name was already registered, the same bitmask is returned. "Normal" always returns zero, "All" always returns full mask.

parameterdescription.para: The unique name for the state

simplesect.para: The ViewState with the assigned bitmask

parameterdescription.para: if no more bits are available (max 62 custom states)

briefdescription.para: ViewState is a value type representing one or more view states as a bitmask.

detaileddescription.para: Predefined states (Focused, Pressed, Disabled, etc.) are registered at static initialization time. Custom states can be created at runtime via Create().
States can be combined with operator+, removed with operator-, and tested with Contains() or HasIntersectionWith().
